# Imajin — The Light Web

> For 2,000 years the money was in the lie. Information asymmetry — I know what you don't, I hide it, I profit from the gap — is the business model under advertising, finance, platforms, and supply chains.
> Imajin is the light web: a sovereign, auditable layer where the signed record IS the value, so honesty becomes the profitable move. Be honest. Make money.
> Services are commodity. Chains are permanent. Identity, attribution, trust, and settlement are structural.

## Full specification

For the complete protocol spec, architecture, and API reference:
https://imajin.ai/llms-full.txt

## The Problem

Every platform you use holds your identity, reputation, and audience hostage. Not because they're evil — because the architecture requires it, and because the architecture pays to keep you in the dark. Imajin fixes this structurally: users hold the keys, the record is signed and portable, and the incentive flips — a system that can't profit by lying to you has to serve you. Identity is self-certifying, reputation is portable, data lives on hardware you own.

## The Inversion

The whole edifice runs on monetized opacity: value extracted from what you can't see. Imajin inverts the *incentive*, not just the tech. Proof-of-history means the record of what really happened is itself the value — so hiding stops paying and disclosure starts. The human is centered not as a slogan but as a consequence. You do a human thing (send a voice note, take a photo, drop a file); an agent infers what you meant, acts on your behalf, asks before anything crosses a boundary, and signs a record of what it did. The machinery recedes; the proof remains.

## What is Imajin?

Imajin (今人 — "now-person") is sovereign infrastructure: one cryptographic kernel plus disposable userspace apps. The kernel provides signed identity chains (DFOS), .fair attribution manifests, and bilateral attestations. Live since February 2026: one kernel, two platform apps, five Imajin apps, three client apps — all real, all self-hostable, open source (MIT).

The kernel enforces identity and settlement. Userspace apps (events, chat, market, learn, etc.) are disposable — swap, rewrite, or add more without touching the chain layer.

## Architecture

```
MJN (L6)     ← identity + attribution + trust + settlement
DFOS (L0–L5) ← proof chains, key rotation, countersignatures
HTTPS        ← transport
```

## The kernel (one app, all core routes)

The kernel (canonical host `jin.imajin.ai`) serves every core capability as route-groups of a single app — not separate deployments:

| Capability | Path | Role |
|------------|------|------|
| auth | jin.imajin.ai/auth | DIDs, sessions, attestations, DFOS bridge |
| pay | jin.imajin.ai/pay | Stripe, settlement, .fair verification |
| connections | jin.imajin.ai/connections | Trust — pods, invites, groups, vouches |
| profile | jin.imajin.ai/profile | Discovery — public profiles, handles |
| registry | jin.imajin.ai/registry | Federation — node discovery, DFOS relay |
| chat | jin.imajin.ai/chat | DID messaging, WebSocket |
| media | jin.imajin.ai/media | Assets, .fair sidecars |
| calendar | jin.imajin.ai/calendar | Scheduling, consent-gated availability |
| mcp | jin.imajin.ai/mcp | Sovereign connector door (OAuth, per-DID scopes) |
| oauth | jin.imajin.ai/oauth | Authorization surface for connectors |

## Userspace apps

Disposable apps on top of the kernel — swap, rewrite, or add more without touching the chain layer:
events, learn, market, coffee, links, dykil.

## Identity

Ed25519 keypairs → `did:imajin:xxx`. Three standing tiers: soft (email), preliminary (keypair), established (earned). Chain-backed via DFOS.

## Key Concepts

- **JBOS** — Just a Bunch Of Services. No orchestration. Services verify chains, not each other.
- **.fair** — JSON manifests: who made it, who gets paid. Kernel primitive — settlement rejects unsigned manifests.
- **Bilateral attestations** — both parties sign via DFOS countersignatures. Trust is mutual, not unilateral.
- **Selective disclosure** — release only what's needed, only with consent, only for that interaction. Every release is logged and auditable.
- **Exit credentials** — leave with your reputation. Signed, portable, encrypted under your key.
- **Trust-gated commerce** — three seller tiers: direct (off-platform settlement), protected (on-platform with .fair), trust-gated (visible only to verified members with preliminary DID+).

## Currency & settlement (there is no token to trade)

The protocol is currency-agnostic. A node can settle in CAD via Stripe, in USD, in community credits, or in MJNx — whatever makes sense for that community. **MJNx is to Imajin as USD is to SWIFT: SWIFT moves money, it doesn't BE money.** The protocol doesn't require the token; the token requires the protocol.

There is no public asset to trade. **MJN is an internal accounting unit (1 MJN = 0.01 CHF), not a tradable token.** Hardware first, token later — Year 3. Roadmap: Year 1 software, Year 2 devices, Year 3 chip.

## Links

- Full spec: https://imajin.ai/llms-full.txt
- Source: https://github.com/ima-jin/imajin-ai
- .fair spec: https://github.com/ima-jin/.fair
- MJN protocol: https://github.com/ima-jin/mjn-protocol
- DFOS: https://protocol.dfos.com
- DFOS relay: https://jin.imajin.ai/registry/relay/
- OpenAPI specs: https://jin.imajin.ai/<capability>/api/spec
- Developer guide: https://imajin.ai/developer-guide

## Build Stats (July 2026)

Live since February 2026 · 2,700+ commits · one kernel, two platform apps, five Imajin apps, three client apps · 1 human + AI agents · single server, no Kubernetes/Docker/service-mesh. The constraint is intentional — if the architecture can't be maintained by a small team, it's the wrong architecture.

## Contact

Ryan Veteze — ryan@imajin.ai
This is exit infrastructure from platform dependency, not a competing platform.
